zh.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX是Ausilent DE COMTOR的女兒，於大約1120年出生在法國奧其坦尼洛艾豪德拉可。她於大約1140年與Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1175年之後在法國奧其坦尼洛艾艾斯塔波斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX是Ausilent DE COMTOR的女儿，于大约1120年出生在法国奧其坦尼洛艾豪德拉可。她于大约1140年与Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1175年之后在法国奧其坦尼洛艾艾斯塔波斯。

nameMeanings.name: DU
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: MAS
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ramon, Francisco, Jose, Juan, Pedro, Jaime, Luis, Carlos, Raul, Ana, Angel, Aquiles.

Catalan and French (Occitan): topographic name for someone who lived in an isolated dwelling in the country, rather than in a village, from Catalan and Occitan mas ‘farmstead’ (from Late Latin mansum, mansus). Compare Dumas .
North German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  . Compare   Maas   1 and   Mass  .

variants.name: Dumas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Michel, Pierre, Antoine, Camille, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Philippe, Raoul.

French: topographic name for someone who lived in an isolated dwelling in the country rather than in a village, from Occitan mas ‘farmstead’ (see Mas 1); or a habitational name for someone from Le Mas, the name of several places mainly in the southern part of France, named with the same word. It is formed with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’. This surname is also common in Haiti. Compare Demos and Demoss .

History: François Dumas from Nanteuil-en-Vallée in Charente, France, married Marguerite Foy in QC in 1667. — This surname is listed (independently and along with its altered forms De Mos and De Moss) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Marquèze
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jesus, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Raul, Ruben, Pedro, Rafael.  Portuguese Ligia, Godofredo.
Spanish (Márquez): patronymic from the personal name Marcos .

variants.name: Marcos
meanings: From Latin Marcus. See Mark .

en.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX was born about 1120, in Chaudeyrac, Lozère, Occitanie, France as the daughter of Ausilent DE COMTOR. She married Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon about 1140. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died after 1175, in Estables, Lozère, Occitanie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX est née environ 1120, à Chaudeyrac, Lozère, Occitanie, France, fille de Ausilent DE COMTOR. Elle a épousé Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon environ 1140. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée après 1175, à Estables, Lozère, Occitanie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX wurde um 1120 in Chaudeyrac, Lozère, Occitanie, Frankreich, als Tochter von Ausilent DE COMTOR geboren. Sie hat um 1140 Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb nach 1175 in Estables, Lozère, Occitanie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX è nata il circa 1120 nel Chaudeyrac, Lozère, Occitanie, Francia come figlia di Ausilent DE COMTOR. Lei sposato Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon circa 1140. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì dopo il 1175, nel Estables, Lozère, Occitanie, Francia.

ja.Châteauneuf-de-Randon,48043,Lozère,Occit: フランスオクシタニーロズeアチaテアウネウフ-デ-ランドン
ja.Estables, Mende, Lozère, Occitanie, Fran: フランスオクシタニーロズeアエスタブレス

ja.Châteauneuf-de-Randon, Lozère, Languedoc: フランスラングエドク-ロウシロンロズeアチaテアウネウフ-デ-ランドン
ja.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUXは約1120年にフランスオクシタニーロズeアチャウデイラックでAusilent DE COMTORの娘として生まれた。約1140年にGuillaume Châteauneuf de Randonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1175年以後にフランスオクシタニーロズeアエスタブレスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX(은)는 대략 1120년 프랑스 옥시탠이 로즈e르 차데이라크에서 Ausilent DE COMTOR의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1140년 Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1175년 이후 프랑스 옥시탠이 로즈e르 에스타블레스에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX nasceu aproximadamente 1120, em Chaudeyrac, Lozère, Occitanie, França filha de Ausilent DE COMTOR. Ela casou-se com Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon aproximadamente 1140. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu após 1175, em Estables, Lozère, Occitanie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX родилась около 1120 г. в местности чаудирак, лозeр, окситани, Франция. Её мать звали Ausilent DE COMTOR. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon около 1140 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла после 1175 г. в местности эстаблесь, лозeр, окситани, Франция.

sm.Abt 1120–Aft 1175: e tusa o le 1120–ina ua ma'ea le 1175

es.lifeSummary: Marquèze DU MAS DE GROSFAUX nació aproximadamente 1120, en Chaudeyrac, Lozère, Occitanie, Francia como hija de Ausilent DE COMTOR. Se casó con Guillaume Châteauneuf de Randon aproximadamente 1140. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió después de 1175, en Estables, Lozère, Occitanie, Francia.
